text.title: testing-backend-languages Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for testing-backend-languages as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Slow, flaky tests from missing let! vs let distinction — let is lazy; let! forces evaluation immediately in before blocks.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Test ordering sensitivity emerges when objects created lazily are mutated by other examples. Use let! for objects that must exist before every example in the group.

failure-mode.description: Agent adds @SpringBootTest to all test classes because the example in documentation uses it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) for services, @DataJpaTest for repositories, @WebMvcTest for controllers in the prompt. Only use @SpringBootTest for explicit integration tests labelled @Tag("integration").

failure-mode.description: Agent uses new Mock without setting up the method under test, leaving it returning null/default.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Untriggered mocks return default values silently; the test passes vacuously. Always verify mock setup covers every call the SUT makes.

failure-mode.description: Agent mixes #[test] with async fn, producing a function that compiles but panics at runtime.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: async fn under #[test] (without tokio::test) creates a future that is never polled — the test body never runs, assertion is never reached, test "passes". Explicitly require #[tokio::test] for all async test functions in the prompt.
